mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 6.0% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the monthly change in UNRATE shows no predictive power for 5-day equity moves over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production growth accelerates while housing starts decline indicates a rotation from rate-sensitive sectors to cyclical industrials, compressing equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO momentum minus HOUST momentum) fails to explain more than 2% of the variance in 20-day SPX returns, or the signal flips direction during high-volatility regimes without regime adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: During a rapid monetary tightening cycle, rising short-term rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress valuation multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions lag rate hikes.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns are not systematically negative when FEDFUNDS increases by >50 basis points over a 3-month window, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS in a multivariate regression becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window as earnings momentum feeds into equity pricing.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day SPX forward returns, or positive GDP surprises consistently fail to produce positive equity returns in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit outstanding declines for two consecutive months, it indicates tightening household balance sheets and reduced consumption capacity, which mechanically pressures retail and consumer discretionary earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Two-month consecutive declines in RSAFS do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in >55% of historical instances, or the signal loses predictive power when controlling for CPIAUCSL inflation surprises.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous surge in nonfarm payrolls growth and a sharp drop in the unemployment rate signals a labor market overheating that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, creating valuation headwinds and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day equity returns, or the interaction effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is held constant.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index relative to historical trends indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and improving supply-side efficiency, which lowers input costs and boosts corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rising INDPRO fails to correlate with positive 5-day SPX returns, or the signal reverses direction during periods of CPIAUCSL inflation exceeding 3.5% annually.
